title: Fixed-monochrome conventional language-support layer
summary: custom.language_support owns source-faithful C#/Python presentation and semantic-color suppression, while nvim-lspconfig, Mason, and the shared LspAttach layer own their conventional language intelligence.
details: language_support.lua declares filetype-to-Tree-sitter-language metadata for cs/c_sharp and python/python rather than widening any C++ frontend filetype set.

It reads each installed highlight query's capture inventory and installs language-qualified links: ordinary captures to Normal, comment captures and string.documentation to Comment, and spell/conceal/control captures untouched.
A bounded asynchronous retry covers the first buffer opened while nvim-treesitter is still compiling a newly ensured parser; normal editor startup never blocks on the presentation layer.

FileType applies monochrome, source-faithful window options, and dependency guidance; BufWinEnter repairs window-local conceal state inherited from another buffer; ColorScheme reapplies capture links.

A dedicated LspAttach hook disables semantic-token presentation per supported buffer. The existing central LspAttach hook remains the sole owner of shared mappings, diagnostics interaction, document highlights, and inlay-hint toggling.
lsp.lua enables roslyn_ls and basedpyright through the current vim.lsp.config/vim.lsp.enable API. Mason owns BasedPyright unconditionally and owns Roslyn conditionally when dotnet can execute.
treesitter.lua ensures c_sharp and python parsers but reserves its custom C/C++/CUDA disable/classic-syntax path for those three frontend languages only.
rationale: One small metadata-driven module prevents duplicated monochrome and source-window lifecycle logic without coupling ordinary languages to the character-changing frontend.
Language-qualified highlight links avoid flattening unrelated buffers process-wide.
Separating presentation from LSP activation makes it explicit that disabling colors does not disable language intelligence.

provenance.note: Entry point and current division between language presentation, parser provisioning, executable provisioning, and server behavior.
Existing Julia fixed-monochrome presentation, C++ buffer gates, global highlight constraints, nvim-lspconfig defaults, and Mason prerequisites were compared before choosing this boundary.

evidence.claim: The complete component boundary is exercised through real FileType/BufWinEnter/LspAttach events, language parsers, capture links, namespace inspection, server configuration, workspace discovery, and live Python navigation.

questions: Which module keeps C# and Python monochrome?
Why are C# and Python absent from dans_mode's frontend buffer set?
How does a first-time C# parser install become monochrome?
Which component installs and enables Roslyn or BasedPyright?

last_verified.method: Static ownership audit and focused real-Neovim parser, presentation, LSP configuration, and navigation coverage.
